english: some evil spirit; γdon _jug-pa (gdon 'jug pa) the entering of a demon into a person; γdón-mi-za-ba (gdon mi za ba) certainty, surety; de byuṅ-ba-la (de byung ba la) or de byuṅ-bar γdon mi za (de byung bar gdon mi za) there is no doubt of such a thing having happened; gen. adv.: γdón-mi-za-bar (gdon mi za bar) undoubtedly, indubitably, γdon-mi-_tsal-bar (gdon mi 'tsal bar) Dzl. id.

Lexx. = rigs-ṅán (rigs ngan), चण्डाल, an outcast, a man of the lowest and most despised caste, still below the dmáṅ-rigs (dmangs rigs). The Tibetan word for this caste was perh. originally dól-pa (dol pa) fisherman, and has afterwards been transferred to all persons that gain their livelihood by the killing of animals, and consequently are despised as professional sinners.

1. fetter, chain; γdos-tág (gdos thag) fetter in a fig. sense, bondage, Thgy. - 2. material existence (?), matter (?). γdos-bćás (gdos bcas), (b)rdos-bćás ((b)rdos bcas), material, corporeal, γdos-bćás-kyi lus (gdos bcas kyi lus) Thgr., frq.; γdos-bćás-su grúb-pa med (gdos bcas su grub pa med) (these things) are nothing material, they have no substance Thgr.; γdos-méd (gdos med) immaterial, unsubstantial; γdós-su čé-ba (gdos su che ba) seems to be the same as γdos-bćás (gdos bcas), and perh. also γdós-pa _dzin (gdos pa 'dzin) Lex. - 3. γdos brgyáb-pa (gdos brgyab pa) C. for W. *k'a kun gyáb-će* (*kha kun gyab ce*), v. rkún-ma (rkun ma).
1. = γdos (gdos) (?) - 2. Cs. mast, sail-yard; acc. to Lexx. something pertaining to a ship; γdós-bu (gdos bu) oar Sch.

1. self, ṅa bdag (nga bdag) for ṅa nyid (nga nyid) Dzl. V2, 14; gen. in the objective case: myself, thyself, one's self; bdag stoṅ γźan smad (bdag stong gzhan smad) to praise one's self, to blame others; bdag sruṅ-ba (bdag srung ba) to devote one's self to solitary contemplation; or as a genit.: bdág-gi (bdag gi) one's own, my, mine; bdág-gi séms-la smad (bdag gi sems la smad) he reproved himself Dzl.; bdág-tu _dzin-pa (bdag tu 'dzin pa); bdag-_dzin (bdag 'dzin) the clinging to the I, the attachment to one's own self, egotism, frq.; bdag daṅ bdág-gir _dzin-pa (bdag dang bdag gir 'dzin pa) attachment to the I and mine S.O.; bdág-tu ltá-ba (bdag tu lta ba) prob. id., Tar. 35, 18, Schf.: Atmaka-theory, bdag-méd-pai čos (bdag med pa'i chos) Tar. 36, 1 the Anātmaka, the contrary; bdag-méd rnám-pa γnyis (bdag med rnam pa gnyis) are mentioned in Thgy., prob. = gáṅ-zág-gi bdag-méd (gang zag gi bdag med), and čós-kyi bdag-méd (chos kyi bdag med) Mil. c. XII.; bdág-gir med (bdag gir med) S.O.; bdag-méd (bdag med) ultimately coincides with stoṅ-pa-nyíd (stong pa nyid), Burn. I., 462 med. In common life, bdag-med (bdag med) is also used for another, *dag-méd-kyi mi* (*dag med kyi mi*) id.; *dag-méd-la ma taṅ* (*dag med la ma tang*) do not give it to another; bdag-γźan (bdag gzhan) I and others, one's self and others; bdag-nyíd (bdag nyid) 1. = bdag (bdag) I myself, thou thyself, he himself, bdag-nyíd-la γsóṅ-čig (bdag nyid la gsong cig) listen to me! Pth.; rgyál-po bdag-nyíd (rgyal po bdag nyid) the king himself Dzl.; k'a-čig ni bdag-nyíd ráb-tu _byúṅ-bar γsol (kha cig ni bdag nyid rab tu 'byung bar gsol) some ask for the permission of becoming priests themselves Dzl.; bdag-nyíd _ba-źig (bdag nyid 'ba' zhig) only for their own persons Thgy. 2. sbst. the thing itself, the substance, the essence, byaṅ-čub-séms-kyi bdag-nyíd yin (byang chub sems kyi bdag nyid yin) I am the essence of bōdhi, the personified bōdhi, says Mil.; túgs-rjei bdag-nyíd dkon-mčog-γsum (thugs rje'i bdag nyid dkon mchog gsum) o grace personified, Triratna! Glr.; the Ommanipadmehūm is saṅs-rgyás tams-čád-kyi dgóṅs-pa tams-čád γčig-tu bsdús-pai bdag-nyíd (sangs rgyas thams cad kyi dgongs pa thams cad gcig tu bsdus pa'i bdag nyid) Glr., i.e. the sum and substance of all the sentences of all the Buddhas concentrated in one word; bdag-nyíd-čén-po (bdag nyid chen po), čé-bai bdag-nyíd (che ba'i bdag nyid) = rdzógs-pai saṅs-rgyás (rdzogs pa'i sangs rgyas) chief Buddha, Sākyathubpa, S.O. - 2. sbst. pronoun, first person, I, eleg., expressing modesty and respect to the hearer or reader, without amounting to our 'my own humble self', v. prán-bu (phran bu); plur. bdág-čag (bdag cag), bdág-rnams (bdag rnams), bdág-čag-rnams (bdag cag rnams), also in a general sense: we mortals Thgy.; bdág-čag tsóṅ-pa-rnams (bdag cag tshong pa rnams) we, these merchants here Dzl. - 3. the I, the ego = gáṅ-zag (gang zag) Was. (269). - 4. master, lord, for bdág-po (bdag po), v. below. - 5. in natural philosophy the element of solid matter; also for air Stg. - bdág-po (bdag po) 1. proprietor, master, lord; bdág-poi sgra (bdag po'i sgra) the syllable pa, as denoting the active agent, i.e. him that has to do with a thing, e.g. rtá-pa (rta pa) (not to be taken as 'definite article' Cs.); thus in many compound words: k'áṅ-bdag (khang bdag), kyim-bdag (khyim bdag) etc.; túgs-rjei bdág-po (thugs rje'i bdag po) lord of grace, Awalokiteswara, Glr. init.; supreme lord, liege-lord, klui bdág-po (klu'i bdag po) = dbáṅ-po (dbang po), rgyál-po (rgyal po); patron. 2. husband, lord, spouse; hence *á-ma dág-po* (*a ma dag po*), or sriṅ-mo dág-po* (*sring mo dag po*), a vulgar and ob-
